info.name: Cold Storage Monitoring System — Complete API Tests
info.description:
Full test suite for the IoT Cold Storage Monitoring System API.

**How to run:**
1. Import this collection + environment file.
2. Select 'Cold Storage - Development' environment.
3. Run via Collection Runner in ORDER — folder by folder.
4. Folder 1 (Auth) must run first as it saves all tokens.

exec: console.log('Server:', r.service, '| Env:', r.environment, '| Uptime:', r.uptime);
body: { mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ email: pm.environment.get('adminEmail'), password: pm.environment.get('adminPassword') }) }
// Login admin to get fresh token, then we'll logout-all for admin to test it
body: { mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ email: pm.environment.get('adminEmail'), password: pm.environment.get('adminPassword') }) }
pm.test('✅ Status 401 — revoked token rejected', () => pm.response.to.have.status(401));
pm.test('✅ All results are online', () => r.data.forEach(d => pm.expect(d.status).to.eql('online')));
pm.test('✅ All results are maintenance', () => r.data.forEach(d => pm.expect(d.status).to.eql('maintenance')));
pm.test('✅ Name updated', () => pm.expect(r.data.name).to.eql('Updated Test Unit'));
pm.test('✅ All results are operator', () => r.data.forEach(u => pm.expect(u.role.name).to.eql('operator')));
pm.test('✅ All returned users are active', () => r.data.forEach(u => pm.expect(u.isActive).to.be.true));
pm.test('✅ All returned users are inactive', () => r.data.forEach(u => pm.expect(u.isActive).to.be.false));
pm.test('✅ Name updated', () => pm.expect(r.data.name).to.eql('Updated Test Operator'));

item.name: [OP] POST /sensors — Temperature HIGH Alert CS001 (T=15.8 > max 8)
[OP] POST /sensors — Temperature LOW Alert CS001 (T=0.5 < min 2)
[OP] POST /sensors — Humidity HIGH Alert CS001 (H=97 > max 95)
[OP] POST /sensors — Humidity LOW Alert CS001 (H=80 < min 85)

exec: pm.test('✅ Active alerts exist from sensor tests', () => pm.expect(r.meta.pagination.total).to.be.at.least(1));
pm.test('✅ All alerts are high severity', () => r.data.forEach(a => pm.expect(a.severity).to.eql('high')));
pm.test('✅ All results are temperature_high', () => r.data.forEach(a => pm.expect(a.alertType).to.eql('temperature_high')));
pm.test('✅ All alerts belong to CS001', () => r.data.forEach(a => pm.expect(a.deviceId).to.eql('CS001')));
pm.test('✅ All returned alerts are resolved', () => r.data.forEach(a => pm.expect(a.status).to.eql('resolved')));
pm.test('✅ At least 1 resolved alert (from resolve test)', () => pm.expect(r.meta.pagination.total).to.be.at.least(1));
pm.test('✅ All returned alerts are acknowledged', () => r.data.forEach(a => pm.expect(a.status).to.eql('acknowledged')));

item.name: [SA] GET /alerts — Get new active alert for delete test

exec: pm.test('✅ Returns array of devices', () => pm.expect(r.data).to.be.an('array'));
pm.test('✅ Each device has health info', () => {
pm.test('✅ Returns array per device', () => pm.expect(r.data).to.be.an('array'));
pm.test('✅ Each item has deviceId and count', () => {
pm.test('✅ Operator sees their 2 devices', () => pm.expect(r.data.length).to.eql(2));
pm.test('✅ CS001 is in list', () => pm.expect(r.data.some(d => d.deviceId === 'CS001')).to.be.true);
pm.test('✅ CS002 is in list', () => pm.expect(r.data.some(d => d.deviceId === 'CS002')).to.be.true);

item.name: ── SETUP: SA assigns CS003 to Viewer ──

exec: pm.test('✅ Status 200 — CS003 assigned to Viewer', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));
pm.test('✅ Operator sees exactly 2 devices', () => pm.expect(r.data.length).to.eql(2));
pm.test('✅ CS003 NOT present', () => pm.expect(r.data.some(d => d.deviceId === 'CS003')).to.be.false);
pm.test('✅ Status 200 — CS001 is assigned to operator', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));
pm.test('✅ Status 403 — CS003 not assigned to operator', () => pm.response.to.have.status(403));
pm.test('✅ Status 200 — Operator can read CS001 sensors', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));
pm.test('✅ Status 403 — CS003 not assigned to operator', () => pm.response.to.have.status(403));
pm.test('✅ Only CS001/CS002 alerts visible to operator', () => {
pm.test('✅ Status 403 — Operator cannot create devices', () => pm.response.to.have.status(403));
pm.test('✅ Status 403 — Operator cannot delete devices', () => pm.response.to.have.status(403));
pm.test('✅ Viewer sees exactly 1 device (CS003)', () => pm.expect(r.data.length).to.eql(1));
pm.test('✅ Status 200 — Viewer can access assigned CS003', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));
pm.test('✅ Status 403 — CS001 not assigned to viewer', () => pm.response.to.have.status(403));
// First ingest a reading for CS003 using SA token so viewer has data
body: { mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ deviceId: 'CS003', temperature: 10.0, humidity: 88, doorStatus: 'closed' }) }
pm.test('✅ Status 200 — Viewer can read CS003 sensors', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));
pm.test('✅ Status 403 — CS001 not assigned to viewer', () => pm.response.to.have.status(403));
pm.test('✅ Status 403 — Viewer cannot ingest sensor data', () => pm.response.to.have.status(403));
pm.test('✅ Status 403 — Viewer cannot create users', () => pm.response.to.have.status(403));
pm.test('✅ Viewer only sees CS003 alerts', () => {
pm.test('✅ No users/vegetables in viewer response', () => {

item.name: ── CLEANUP: SA removes CS003 from Viewer ──

exec: pm.test('✅ Status 200 — CS003 removed from Viewer', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));
pm.test('✅ Admin sees ALL devices (not just assigned)', () => pm.expect(r.meta.pagination.total).to.be.at.least(5));
pm.test('✅ CS001, CS002, CS003 all present', () => {
pm.test('✅ Name updated', () => pm.expect(r.data.name).to.eql('Admin Test Unit Updated'));

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Admin Broccoli",
  "description": "Created by admin in Postman tests",
  "temperature": { "min": 0, "max": 5 },
  "humidity": { "min": 85, "max": 95 },
  "storageDurationDays": 14
}

exec: pm.test('✅ Admin can list all users', () => pm.expect(r.meta.pagination.total).to.be.at.least(4));
pm.test('✅ Name updated by admin', () => pm.expect(pm.response.json().data.name).to.eql('Admin Updated Viewer'));

item.name: [AD] POST /users/:id/devices — Admin assigns CS001 to user
[AD] DELETE /users/:id/devices — Admin removes device from user

exec: pm.test('✅ Admin can list all alerts', () => pm.expect(pm.response.json().data).to.be.an('array'));
pm.test('✅ Admin sees health for all devices (5+)', () => pm.expect(pm.response.json().data).to.be.an('array').with.length.at.least(5));
pm.test('✅ Admin gets alerts by device', () => pm.expect(pm.response.json().data).to.be.an('array'));
pm.test('✅ Admin reads CS001 sensor data', () => pm.expect(pm.response.json().data.deviceId).to.eql('CS001'));
pm.test('✅ Admin can ingest sensor data', () => pm.expect(r.data.reading.temperature).to.eql(4.5));
